codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of session and process group management APIs. All 5 functions (iox_setsid, iox_getsid, iox_getpgrp, iox_getpgid, iox_setpgid) are correctly implemented with proper Linux semantics, error handling (ESRCH, EPERM), and 11 unit tests covering positive and negative cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Code follows naming conventions consistently: iox_ prefix for functions, IOX_ for macros, proper errno setting pattern. All functions validate input and set errno appropriately.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-system/kernel/task/task.c:198-313 - All functions follow iox_ naming, set errno=ESRCH for invalid tasks and errno=EPERM for permission violations

sharedStateObservations.observation: Build and test infrastructure works correctly. Tests run via ./build-macos/ixland-system/iox-core-tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows build succeeded and all 18 session/process group tests passed

summary: Feature 'process-table-sessions' is complete and correctly implemented. The worker implemented all 5 session/process group APIs (setsid, getsid, getpgrp, getpgid, setpgid) with proper Linux semantics including: (1) setsid creates new session with sid=pgid=pid and returns EPERM if already group leader, (2) getsid returns session ID with ESRCH for invalid PIDs, (3) getpgrp/getpgid return process group IDs with proper zero-pid handling, (4) setpgid moves processes between groups with EPERM for non-child/non-self targets and cross-session moves. All 11 unit tests pass, covering positive cases and error conditions. Implementation fulfills VAL-PROCESS-026, VAL-PROCESS-027, and VAL-PROCESS-028.
